Renotice Of Plaintiff Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris, & Mcsparrans Notice Of And Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Courts Discovery Order, Including Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris, & McSparran's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Courts Discovery Order, Including Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defense counsel complied with the portion of my prior order relating to sanctions. The Defendants did not comply regarding discovery but had good cause: specifically, Michael was hospitalized and Ellen was caring for him. While it would have been better practice for Defendants to seek relief from the order based on these circumstances, imposing terminating sanctions would be unjust. CCP 2023.030; Dept. of Forestry v. Howell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 154, 191 ("terminating sanctions are to be used sparingly because of the drastic effect of their application"). Nevertheless, Defendants still have not complied with the prior order. They are ordered to provide full, complete, and code-compliant discovery responses, with verifications, no later than August 27, 2021, or such other date as the parties may agree. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,560 (half the requested fee to account for partial success, plus the filing fee), payable by August 27, 2021.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
